title: ED domestic violence screening documentation

examination.text: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.
examination.rationale: The exact history and examination section supports recording focused examination and general appearance only when consented to and performed; it does not mandate a sexual-assault examination.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook

exact_section.heading: History and physical examination after sexual violence

investigations.text: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.
investigations.rationale: Recognition and clinically indicated direct enquiry were reviewed; the source does not support universal screening.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.

exact_section.heading: Summary and Recognition of Domestic Abuse

treatment_and_management.text: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact WHO documentation section supports a consent-based record of complaints, symptoms, signs or injuries, completed risk discussion and clinician-entered plan only when actually discussed.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook

referral.text: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.
referral.rationale: The exact RCEM disclosure section supports recording safeguarding concern, specialist discussion and handover or follow-up only when disclosure occurred and actions were completed.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.

exact_section.heading: Disclosure, injury records, child welfare and support

safety_netting.text: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.
safety_netting.rationale: The exact WHO first-line-support section supports documenting the person’s concerns or goals, associated needs, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed safety context only when discussed; it does not assert violence or mandate action.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

source_limitations: Population beyond adult women, safe interpreter use, perpetrator relationship, children/dependants, immediate danger, coercive control, sexual violence, strangulation, pregnancy, injury details, forensic examination, reporting law and local referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
